overview: This white/red Eldrazi variant leverages mana acceleration and artifact denial to establish early board presence and disrupt opponent strategies. By utilizing Lotus Petal to cast Chalice of the Void and Kozilek's Command ahead of schedule, the deck aims to control the pace while generating incremental value. The Kozilek token serves as a flexible win condition that can attack, block, or be sacrificed for card advantage and removal.
win_conditions: Kozilek token attacking for lethal damage
Drawing into game-winning spells via Kozilek's Command
Locking opponent mana with Chalice of the Void to force unfavorable trades

keep_criteria: Hands containing Lotus Petal and Chalice of the Void
Hands with Kozilek's Command and early interaction
Hands with three or more lands and a one-drop
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking artifact interaction or mana acceleration
Hands with four or more lands
Hands without Kozilek's Command or Chalice

early_game: Play Lotus Petal to enable turn-one Chalice of the Void or Kozilek's Command
Set Chalice at zero or one to disrupt fast aggro and combo strategies
Use Kozilek's Command to draw cards or destroy early threats
mid_game: Stabilize the board by maintaining Chalice pressure and generating Kozilek tokens
Trade efficiently with opponent's creatures while preserving card advantage
Begin applying pressure with tokens or creatures to force opponent to respond
late_game: Outvalue the opponent through repeated Kozilek's Command activations and card draw
Use Chalice to lock out late-game finishers or sweepers
Close out games with accumulated resources and board presence

key_cards.name: Lotus Petal
key_cards.notes: Play on turn one to cast Chalice of the Void or Kozilek's Command ahead of schedule, enabling early disruption and value generation.

key_cards.name: Chalice of the Void
key_cards.notes: Enter with X=0 or X=1 to counter fast spells and disrupt mana, forcing opponents into inefficient plays while protecting your board.

key_cards.name: Kozilek's Command
key_cards.role: Card advantage, removal, and win condition
key_cards.notes: Use to draw cards, destroy problematic permanents, or produce a Kozilek token that can attack, block, or be sacrificed for additional value.

sequencing_tips: Drop Chalice of the Void before the opponent's turn to maximize disruption
Use Lotus Petal to cast Kozilek's Command on turn one or two for immediate impact
Hold up mana to counter spells if Chalice is already on the battlefield
Prioritize drawing cards with Kozilek's Command when behind on resources

matchups.advice: Set Chalice at zero or one to slow down early creatures and use Kozilek's Command for targeted removal.
Set Chalice at two to disrupt mid-range spells and use Kozilek's Command to draw into answers and race with tokens.
Set Chalice at zero or one to disrupt key combo pieces and use Kozilek's Command to draw into disruption or clear the board.
Balance Chalice settings between one and two, use Kozilek's Command to trade efficiently, and race with tokens.
